title: How to Image Trace a Low-Contrast Sketch
description: Sometimes sketches are too faint to get a decent image trace in Adobe Illustrator. Here are some steps you can take to prep your sketch for better tracing, and some ideal settings in Image Trace to get the best results.

steps.title: Step One
steps.text:
In Photoshop

1. Open your sketch in Photoshop.
2. In the Adjustments panel, add a new Gradient Map adjustment layer.

steps.title: Step Two
steps.text:
Add a Gradient Map adjustment layer over your sketch and dial in the settings to make it suited for Image Tracing.

Once your sketch is ready, save it out for importing into Illustrator.

steps.title: Step Three
steps.text:
In Illustrator

1. Drag your new level-adjusted sketch into Illustrator.
2. With the image selected, click the Image Trace button in the Control Menu, or the contextual menu bar under the image.

steps.title: Step Four
steps.text: Open the Image Trace Panel to fine-tune the settings.

steps.title: Step Five
steps.text: Play with these particular Image Trace settings to dial in the best capture of the sketch.

steps.title: Step Six
steps.text: Set your file up for easy vector coloring.
